Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

I don't think we're in a hurry to land this functionality to b2_10.  This is a feature/performance improvement, but I'd prefer to let it soak on master/2.11 for some time to ensure it is working correctly, since this has the potential to cause data corruption.

Patch Set 2:

Agreed strongly.  I don't really think this is a candidate for b2_10 at all.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to b2_10.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 2:

Minh,

I assume you want to abandon this.

parents.subject: LU-10663 utils: clear errno before check

commit.subject: LU-9409 llite: Add tiny write support
commit.message:
LU-9409 llite: Add tiny write support

If a page is already dirty in the page cache, we can write
to it without a full i/o.  This improves performance for
writes of < 1 page dramatically.

Append writes are a bit tricky, requiring us to take the
range lock (which we can normally avoid), but they are
still much faster than the normal i/o path.

Performance numbers with dd, on a VM with an older Xeon.

All numbers in MiB/s.

                8 bytes 1KiB
Without patch:  .75     75
With patch:     6.5     153

Lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/27903
Lustre-commit: 94470f7eeab5fde0648a14dda36941402c6a3e10

Cray-bug-id: LUS-1705
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Change-Id: I75cc72ceb5f174a5394af8ffe5df4fe9583f19a3
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Signed-off-by: Minh Diep <minh.diep@intel.com>
